title: Odd Even Linked List
description:
Odd Even Linked List

Given the head of a singly linked list, group all the nodes with odd indices together followed by the nodes with even indices, and return the reordered list.

The first node is considered odd, and the second node is even, and so on.

Note that the relative order inside both the even and odd groups should remain as it was in the input.

You must solve the problem in O(1) extra space complexity and O(n) time complexity.

Example 1

Input: head = [1,2,3,4,5]
Output: [1,3,5,2,4]

Example 2

Input: head = [2,1,3,5,6,4,7]
Output: [2,3,6,7,1,5,4]

Constraints

  • The number of nodes in the linked list is in the range [0, 104].
  • -106 <= Node.val <= 106
template:
# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val, next):
#         self.val = val
#         self.next = next

def oddEvenList(head):
solution_code:
def oddEvenList(head):
    if not head:
        return head
    odd = head
    even = head.next
    even_head = even
    while even and even.next:
        odd.next = even.next
        odd = odd.next
        even.next = odd.next
        even = even.next
    odd.next = even_head
    return head
solution_text:
Approach: Two-Pointer Weaving

Maintain two pointers: odd (starts at head) and even (starts at head.next). Save even_head so we can reconnect at the end.

Each iteration skips one node for each pointer — odd.next jumps over the current even node, and even.next jumps over the next odd node. When the even pointer reaches the end, link odd.next = even_head to join both chains.

Everything happens in-place with no extra nodes allocated.

Time: O(n)   Space: O(1)
verify:
def verify(actual_output, expected_output):
    def list_to_array(head):
        result = []
        current = head
        while current:
            result.append(current.val)
            current = current.next
        return result

    actual_array = list_to_array(actual_output)
    passed = actual_array == expected_output
    return [passed, str(actual_array)]
